title: Chapter Eighty-Five: Battle for Possession (Part Four)
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

They took turns on watch. The Seventh Apostle smiled from three hundred zhang out, a red smear in the distance, patient as a circling hawk.

On a cloudy night, Yeon-shin drew the Desolate Sword.

The stars had buried themselves in darkness. The moon wore grey clouds like a veil. Only dreamlike moonlight descended. He recalled Cheong Myeong's footwork -- the way the Elf moved like water over stone -- and began his approach.

He ran silently, seeping into the night air.

*She's sleeping.*

He found her on a thick tree branch, red robe pulled below her shoulders like a blanket, curled up like a child. He did not hesitate. He stepped with supreme footwork, true qi exploding from the bubbling well point on his sole, and launched.

At the apex of his soaring movement, a gale arose.

An instant. He rose to her eye level. Her long breath touched his cheek. Their eyes met. Red pupils, slightly opened, hidden by small eye-smiles.

*Expected.*

He struck down with his right hand's swift sword while raising left arm true qi. The fourth technique -- unnamed still. Simultaneous operation of Demon Roaring Blood Art and Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm.

Her foot flew out. Kicking while lying sideways -- fast, impossibly fast. Her right foot extending as if crushing empty air pushed back from the sword blade.

He thrust his left palm at her face and burst it.

The tree shattered. The rebound slammed through his shoulder blade. His body was in mid-air -- he bounced like a leaf in a typhoon, landed smoothly, sheathed the Desolate Sword in one hazy motion.

"Your techniques are becoming more refined day by day," came the languid voice from above. "Amazing, our Grand Teacher."

She had avoided even the palm technique.

He recited the fifth and sixth verses of Demon Roaring Blood Technique. What he owed.

Then turned away.

In the distance, Hwa-sin stood at the treetop with arms crossed -- silhouette against hazy moonlight, guarding against treachery. He descended with accomplished body technique.

"Indeed, your techniques are becoming more precise. It feels like witnessing the birth of divine techniques."

"I'm not sure if I can complete it within the remaining fourteen strikes."

"A supreme master able to face the main fortress' black ranks. Making life-and-death exchanges daily -- whatever the result, your gains won't be small."

"I'm grateful you always accommodate me. Getting caught up in my grudge."

Sweat on Hwa-sin's palm, visible. He had been gripping his sword the whole time.

Later, walking back to camp, Yeon-shin spoke what was on his mind.

"The person you called sister. Flourishing Spirit Flower Master Namgung Mi. Tell me."

"Actually it's nothing special." The bitter smile. "The issue was that my mother's status was an assassin raised by the main family."

Yeon-shin said nothing. He listened.

"A place called Sword Night handles tasks difficult for the well-known Azure Sky Sword Heaven Group. Assassinations. Kidnappings. My mother was a Sword Night assassin who one day caught the family head's eye." Hwa-sin's voice stayed even. "Thus I was born. My qualities were decent. The family head wanted to make this illegitimate son the leader of Sword Night. He had spirit medicine given to an illegitimate child -- which earned me my direct line sister's hatred."

"You mean the Flourishing Spirit Flower Master. Your current martial match opponent."

"Yes. She inflicted all sorts of humiliation on my mother. Though my mother seemed not to even think of such things. She was born an assassin after all." He paused. "I heard news of mother's death two years ago. She went to assassinate a Thirteen Evil Sects Lord's direct descendant -- an absurd mission. The Flourishing Spirit Flower Master's scheme. She had secretly stamped the family head's proxy seal."

"Document forgery? Isn't that something even third-rate factions wouldn't do?"

"The family was turned upside down. The Flourishing Spirit Flower Master received punishment of hundred days closed door training. She went in smiling." His raised hand shook violently. "Just imagining cutting my sister's neck."

A life of oppression revealed as trembling.

Yeon-shin did not speak. He knew solitude. He empathised through silence.

That was enough. Hwa-sin smiled with gratitude and leaned against a tree.

---

At Wuchang's post station, they rested and planned. Two days of watching. The post station servant -- ten copper coins and bright-eyed -- brought reports of Namgung Mi's response.

She had destroyed the dantians of the Virtuous Teacher Sect servants she had left alive. Damaged their mute points to steal their speech. Then took the stage again, shedding tears pitifully, playing the heavenly maiden.

"They were servants of evil," she had told the crowd. "Even if you point fingers saying there was no mercy in my methods, I will humbly endure it."

No one blamed her. The influence of her speech was great. Relatives of the mutilated servants could only watch those who cheered.

Namgung Mi's fame was rising endlessly.

*Desolate Sect is a group that must always gain public sentiment.*

"This would be a problem even if the main fortress wins the martial matches," Hwa-sin said, his expression heavy.

"If we take their heads in life-and-death matches, at least in this vicinity Desolate Sect would earn public resentment."

Silence.

"There's no reason to hastily respond to this battle for justification. The elders will handle it. We'll receive recognition for doing what we can."

"Young Hero Jeong...?"

"Isn't it common to say both sides can benefit in any dispute?"

Yeon-shin rummaged through his travel pack. The feeling that met his hand was chillingly soft. Thousand silkworm thread. He pulled out a robe red as blood.

The Seventh Apostle's pure blood robe.

Hwa-sin stared.

"We just need one mask," Yeon-shin said. "A demon mask would be good."

"Surely not?"

"Young Hero Namgung." The boy held up the crimson robe. Firelight caught it and the silk seemed to move of its own accord, alive with old violence. "Please tell me. Would it be alright to show no mercy in our methods?"

The question hung in the dim room like smoke.

Outside, the night pressed close against the walls of Wuchang, and somewhere in that darkness, three hundred zhang distant, a red-eyed woman smiled in her sleep.
